articles.title: Iran-Israel war LIVE: Trump rejects Iran’s peace proposal as 'totally unacceptable'
articles.description: Follow The Hindu's live blog for the latest updates on the Iran war on May 11, 2026, as Washington awaits Tehran’s response to a U.S. peace proposal

articles.title: Under-threat British PM Starmer to attempt reset after disastrous polls
articles.description: British PM Keir Starmer seeks a leadership reset after poor election results, facing internal party pressure and public discontent.

articles.title: 'A black swan event, good thing that it happened': Annamalai on TVK's win in debut Tamil Nadu polls
articles.description: BJP leader K. Annamalai praised the win of Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK), asserting that the subsequent defeat of traditional Dravidian parties in the 2026 Tamil Nadu Assembly Election has emerged as a 'black swan event' for the state. Speaking at a roundtable during the Stanford India Conference, Annamalai said the outcome reflects a 'rupture of the system,' comparing it to disruptive change in startup ecosystems. He argued that entrenched political structures had become overloaded over time and suggested that both regional and national parties had struggled to adapt beyond traditional strongholds. According to him, such an electoral outcome, though unexpected, marks a significant turning point that will shape the state's political trajectory in the coming years.

articles.title: Iran Rejects Trump’s ‘Surrender’ Proposal, Demands Full Hormuz Control And War Reparations
articles.description: Iran has rejected a U.S. proposal for renewed peace talks, calling it “surrender” and demanding war reparations, full control of the Strait of Hormuz, sanctions relief, and the release of seized assets. Donald Trump dismissed Tehran’s response as “TOTALLY UNACCEPTABLE,” sending oil prices higher and reviving fears of a deeper Middle East conflict. The Hormuz crisis continues to threaten global shipping and energy markets as Israel warns operations against Iran are not over. With drones detected over Gulf states and diplomacy stalling, tensions across the region remain dangerously high amid mounting fears of economic shockwaves worldwide.#Iran #USIran #Trump #Hormuz #MiddleEast #OilPrices #Netanyahu #IsraelIranWar #GlobalOil #BreakingNews #IranNews #StraitOfHormuz #EnergyCrisis #WorldNews #Tehran

articles.title: Trisha Cries As Thalapathy Vijay Takes Oath As Tamil Nadu CM | Emotional Reaction Goes Viral
articles.description: Trisha Krishnan became emotional during Vijay’s oath-taking ceremony as Tamil Nadu CM. Videos of the actress fighting back tears went viral.

articles.description: Netanyahu shied away from sharing military plans and a timeline regarding that operation but described it as “terrifically important mission”.

articles.description: US President Donald Trump will visit China from May 13 to 15 for talks with Xi Jinping focused on Iran, trade and broader geopolitical tensions.

articles.title: Russia offered India ‘Satellite Killer’ MiG-31 – Why did India say no?
articles.description: Even today, MiG-31 is one of the fastest operational combat aircraft and continues to be used in specialised roles by Russia and Kazakhstan. Its relevance in modern warfare is not only about the aircraft, but also the weapons it carries and the long-range interception concept it represents.

articles.title: Israel Gets $3.8 Billion US Military Aid A Year. Netanyahu Wants It To Be 0
articles.description: The US has agreed to provide a total of $38 billion in military aid to Israel from 2018 to 2028.

articles.title: Fadnavis echoes PM Modi, warns of fuel scarcity amid global tensions
articles.description: Maha CM Devendra Fadnavis echoed PM Modi's appeal for responsible consumption, warning that the war involving America, Israel, and Iran could lead to fuel shortages in India if resources are not used properly.

articles.title: Delhi Confidential: Investment ‘dhandapani’
articles.description: Dhandapani, who has been handling Gandhi’s investments since 2013, said he has been a “strong supporter” of Prime Minister Narendra Modi “from 2014 to 2024” but that has not affected their working relationship.

articles.description: Asansol: Daily worship resumed at Asansol's Bastin Bazar Durga Mandir on Sunday — Mother's Day — fulfilling a poll promise made during the campaign, i.

articles.title: MEA makes a new push for database on students abroad
articles.description: The move comes in the wake of the recent Russia-Ukraine and US-Iran conflicts, when a large number of Indian students reached out to the missions and had to be evacuated to safety.

articles.title: Trump and Xi set for talks spanning Iran, nuclear, trade and AI
articles.description: The summit will be the two leaders' first face-to-face talks in more than six months as they try to stabilise ties strained by trade, the US and Israeli war with Iran and other areas of disagreement.

articles.description: Civic polls after six years; hopes of ward-level accountability revived

articles.title: Revamp of state BJP imminent, strategy to be aligned with electoral challenges
articles.description: While state presidents changed repeatedly over the past several years, faces in key organisational posts remained the same since 2010.

articles.title: Iran releases Nobel laureate Narges Mohammadi on bail amid health fears
articles.description: Iranian Nobel Peace Prize winner Narges Mohammadi has been released on bail and transferred to Tehran for urgent medical treatment after supporters warned she was at risk of dying in prison. The activist, jailed for campaigning against Iran's clerical system, reportedly suffered two suspected heart attacks in custody.

articles.title: Udhayanidhi Stalin congratulates new CM Vijay, vows strong opposition
articles.description: Udhayanidhi Stalin congratulated new Tamil Nadu CM C. Joseph Vijay, whose TVK party ended the DMK/AIADMK's dominance. Stalin vowed the DMK would be a powerful opposition, while Vijay promised a 'new era of real, secular and social justice'.

articles.title: Himanta to take oath as Assam CM today after governor’s appointment
articles.description: Himanta Biswa Sarma has been appointed Assam's chief minister by governor Lakshman Prasad Acharya, marking the NDA's third consecutive term. Following his unanimous election as leader of the BJP-led NDA legislature party, Sarma staked his claim with support from 102 MLAs. He will be sworn in on May 12, with Prime Minister Modi expected to attend.

articles.title: Yogi expands ministry, balances caste and regional equations

articles.description: Live broadcast of Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay's swearing-in ceremony drew crowds across 23 Chennai locations on Sunday.

articles.description: Even a single nuclear weapon detonated at high altitude over the United States poses an existential threat to most, if not all, Americans.

articles.description: Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay reviews Tamil Nadu's law and order with officials, focusing on women's safety and drug offenses.

articles.description: CREDAI Tamil Nadu urges transparent governance and swift urban development to meet citizens’ expectations for accountability and progress.

articles.description: Ex-Minister K.R. Periakaruppan claims a postal vote error due to duplicate constituency names cost him the election by one vote.

articles.description: CITU Kollam threatens protests against the ousting of contract workers, calling actions inhumane and politically motivated.

articles.description: ​Joseph Vijay began his tenure as Tamil Nadu chief minister with a nine-member TVK cabinet featuring veteran leaders, first-time faces, professionals, and close aides.

articles.title: Congress panels adopt resolution recommending B.J. Vijay Kumar’s nomination to Legislative Council
articles.description: Congress committees endorse B.J. Vijay Kumar's nomination to the Legislative Council, recognizing his dedicated service over 25 years.

articles.title: Pak minister calls himself ‘ordinary worker’ of Asim Munir, draws ire on social media
articles.description: Pakistan politics: Minister Ali Pervaiz Malik called himself an ordinary worker of Field Marshal Asim Munir, triggering criticism over the military's influence in civilian governance. Social media users accused the ruling PML-N of undermining democracy.

articles.title: Women protest outside Petbasheerabad police station seeking arrest of Bandi Sanjay’s son in POCSO case
articles.description: Women protest in Hyderabad demanding the arrest of Bandi Sanjay’s son in a serious POCSO case involving a minor.

articles.title: Satheesan-Venugopal Face-Off Delays Congress Decision On Kerala Chief Minister
articles.description: The Congress leadership is facing mounting pressure over the selection of Kerala’s next Chief Minister despite the UDF’s landslide victory. Rahul Gandhi and Mallikarjun Kharge held key meetings with contenders V. D. Satheesan, K. C. Venugopal and Ramesh Chennithala. While many MLAs reportedly back Venugopal, public sentiment and alliance partners appear to favour Satheesan. The party is now trying to build consensus while avoiding factional fallout. Watch

articles.title: Udhayanidhi Stalin is the Leader of Opposition
articles.description: Udhayanidhi Stalin elected Leader of the DMK Legislature Party, set to oppose Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay in the Assembly.

articles.title: 3.82 cr devotees take dips at Sangam on Mauni Amavasya; Akhilesh says probe seer's 'mistreatment'
articles.description: Over 3.82 crore devotees performed holy dips at the Sangam and Ganga on Mauni Amavasya during the Magh Mela in Prayagraj. The event saw a brief disruption when police stopped Shankaracharya Swami Avimukteshwaranand Saraswati from proceeding to the Sangam, sparking a political debate over the alleged mistreatment of seers.

articles.title: In Kerala, nearly half of Congress’ MLA-elects are young faces
articles.description: Nearly half of Congress' elected MLAs in Kerala are young leaders, reflecting successful youth engagement in recent elections.

articles.title: ‘A parasitic party’: PM Modi targets Congress over DMK fallout, says it is latching onto Vijay’s TVK in Tamil Nadu
articles.description: At a rally in Bengaluru, PM Modi used the recent BJP electoral wins to contrast "pro-incumbency" with the Congress’s history of losing states, and attacked the party's "power-hungry" politics in Tamil Nadu.

articles.title: From Thalapathy To Tamil Nadu CM: 5 Things To Know About Vijay
articles.description: Tamil star and political figure Vijay, married to Sangeeta Sornalingam since 1999 with two children, faces divorce after she alleged his extramarital affair

articles.title: India, Africa Enter A New Strategic Phase With Focus On Innovation, Trade And Growth
articles.description: India and Africa are preparing for 4th India-Africa Forum Summit in New Delhi from May 28-31, 2026. The meeting aims to speed up cooperation in digital technology, trade, health, agriculture, space, and skills. The partnership focuses on shared development goals, innovation, climate action, and stronger people-to-people ties for inclusive growth.

articles.title: Satheesan, Venugopal, Chennithala: Congress’ Musical Chairs In Kerala Has Just One Chair
articles.description: AICC in-charge for Kerala Deepa Dasmunshi has said the Congress high command will decide "very soon" on who the party's chief minister would be.

articles.title: Historic chapter begins in Tamil Nadu as Vijay's cabinet takes oath
articles.description: After TVK's historic win, Vijay took oath as Tamil Nadu's new CM. Minister KG Arunraj called it a 'historic chapter', outlining priorities like clean governance, women's safety, and a drug-free state, starting with a white paper on finances.

articles.title: Stalin congratulates CM Vijay, cautions him on 'empty treasury' claims
articles.description: Former CM MK Stalin congratulated new Tamil Nadu CM Vijay but warned him against blaming the previous government for the state's financial condition, urging him to fulfil his electoral promises instead of making 'empty treasury' claims.

articles.title: Himanta Biswa Sarma elected NDA leader, AGP calls it 'good fortune'
articles.description: Asom Gana Parishad (AGP) president Atul Bora called it 'good fortune' that Himanta Biswa Sarma was elected leader of the BJP and NDA Legislature Party in Assam. Sarma said the new NDA government will take oath on May 12 with PM Modi present.

articles.title: AAP minister slams Centre's 'Operation Lotus' after ED arrests leader
articles.description: AAP minister Harbhajan Singh ETO slammed the Centre after the ED arrested his colleague Sanjeev Arora, alleging central agencies are being misused for 'Operation Lotus'. Arora was arrested in a money laundering case and remanded to ED custody.

articles.title: P Senthilkumar, G Laxmi Priya appointed secretaries to new TN CM Vijay
articles.description: The Tamil Nadu Government has appointed P Senthilkumar as Additional Chief Secretary/Secretary-I and G Laxmi Priya as Secretary-II to the newly elected Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay, with their posts declared equivalent to senior cadre positions.

articles.title: TVK govt to publish white papers, promises transparent governance: Minister
articles.description: TVK minister R Nirmalkumar promises transparent governance with white papers for all departments after actor Vijay took oath as Tamil Nadu CM. Vijay vowed to address state debt and usher in an era of secularism and social justice.
